data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T230357-ea29fa4d/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T230357-ea29fa4d/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T230357-ea29fa4d/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T230357-ea29fa4d/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T230357-ea29fa4d/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ce007f14">
    <title>fix(escalation): reviewer transport/empty errors escalate reviewer tier</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  When a reviewer invocation fails with OutcomeReviewTransport,
  OutcomeReviewContextOverflow, or returns an empty/unparseable
  body, the bead retries with the SAME reviewer model (and same
  MinPower). After DefaultReviewMaxRetries (3) the bead parks to
  status=proposed.

  This violates ADR-024 P3 (reviewer always stronger than
  implementer) under fault conditions. Repeated transport errors
  on a same-tier reviewer should bump reviewer MinPower to escape
  whatever pool/instance is failing, not retry into the same
  failing context.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go reviewerDispatchProfile
    (line ~697-736) reads MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 once,
    then dispatches; review-error retry reuses the same MinPower.
  - There is no "bump reviewer MinPower on consecutive errors"
    logic in the review dispatch path.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go reviewerDispatchProfile:
    when CountPriorReviewErrorsForSlot > 0 for the same result_rev,
    add another ladder step to MinPowerOverride.
  - On second review-error, reviewer dispatches at impl.ActualPower
    + 2 (or top-of-ladder, whichever is lower).
  - On third review-error, reviewer dispatches at top-of-ladder.
  - On fourth review-error: park to status=proposed (current
    behavior).
  - Emit "reviewer-escalated" event when a bump occurs, with the
    new MinPower and the reviewer-error count.

  Tests:
     - TestReviewerEscalation_OnSecondTransportError: assert
       MinPower bump after first transport error.
     - TestReviewerEscalation_OnThirdError_TopOfLadder: assert
       reviewer reaches top tier after two errors.
     - TestReviewerEscalation_PairingDegraded_TriggersEscalation:
       when ReviewPairingDegraded fires (impl and reviewer at same
       tier), the next reviewer dispatch bumps a tier.

NON-SCOPE
  - Reviewer dispatch concurrency (existing two-slot pattern stays).
  - Adjusting DefaultReviewMaxRetries (out of scope).
  - Implementer escalation on review_block (sister bead R1).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR-024)" P3.
  - The existing ReviewPairingDegraded event (telemetry) gains a
    causal link to escalation.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go reviewerDispatchProfile bumps MinPower when CountPriorReviewErrorsForSlot > 0 for the same result_rev.
2. Each subsequent review-error retry adds another ladder step until top-of-ladder.
3. A "reviewer-escalated" event is emitted on each bump.
4. TestReviewerEscalation_OnSecondTransportError, TestReviewerEscalation_OnThirdError_TopOfLadder, TestReviewerEscalation_PairingDegraded_TriggersEscalation all pass.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:fix, spec:ADR-024, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="cfa2adde11fd7a927306223d426f88ea19798761" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T230357-ea29fa4d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All three tests pass and the full suite is green.

**Summary of all ACs:**

1. ✅ `reviewerDispatchProfile` in `execute_bead_review.go` bumps `MinPower` when `countPriorEscalationTriggers > 0` for the same `result_rev` — each prior error (review-error or review-pairing-degraded) adds one ladder step
2. ✅ Each subsequent review-error retry adds another ladder step until top-of-ladder (2+ errors → `SelectStrongestProfile`, no floor restriction)
3. ✅ A `"reviewer-escalated"` event is emitted via `r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent` on each bump, carrying `new_min_power`, `error_count`, and `result_rev`
4. ✅ All three required tests pass: `TestReviewerEscalation_OnSecondTransportError`, `TestReviewerEscalation_OnThirdError_TopOfLadder`, `TestReviewerEscalation_PairingDegraded_TriggersEscalation`
5. ✅ `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` green
6. ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit` passes
